title: Ephemerantha labangensis
intro: Ephemerantha labangensis, also known as Labang, is a plant species in the family Orchidaceae, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. Traditionally, Labang has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and digestive ailments, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Labang include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Labang include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections in traditional medicine.
taxonomy: Ephemerantha labangensis belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This classification places it within the plant kingdom as an orchid species. The genus Ephemerantha is a monotypic genus, containing only one species. The Orchidaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. As a member of this family, Ephemerantha labangensis exhibits unique characteristics and growth habits.
